name: APScheduler
what_it_does: In-process task scheduler for Python — schedule Python functions to run at specific times, intervals, or cron expressions. APScheduler features: BackgroundScheduler (runs in background thread), BlockingScheduler, AsyncIOScheduler, four trigger types (date, interval, cron, calendarinterval), job stores (memory, SQLAlchemy, MongoDB, Redis), executors (thread pool, process pool, asyncio, gevent), add_job()/remove_job()/pause_job(), misfire_grace_time for late execution, coalesce for missed jobs, scheduler.start()/shutdown(), and REST API for APScheduler 4.x. In-process alternative to cron for Python applications.
use_cases: Agent periodic task — from apscheduler.schedulers.background import BackgroundScheduler; scheduler = BackgroundScheduler(); scheduler.add_job(sync_agent_data, 'interval', minutes=5, id='data_sync'); scheduler.start() — run agent data sync every 5 minutes in background thread; no cron daemon needed; job runs inside Python process

Agent cron-based scheduling — scheduler.add_job(daily_report, 'cron', hour=8, minute=0, timezone='US/Eastern') — run daily report at 8 AM Eastern; agent scheduled reports with cron expression syntax; timezone-aware scheduling; cron trigger supports standard cron fields (second, minute, hour, day, month, day_of_week)

Agent one-time delayed execution — from datetime import datetime, timedelta; scheduler.add_job(send_followup, 'date', run_date=datetime.now() + timedelta(hours=24), args=[user_id]) — schedule callback 24 hours from now; agent follow-up reminders, delayed notifications; date trigger fires exactly once

Agent persistent job store — from apscheduler.jobstores.sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemyJobStore; jobstores = {'default': SQLAlchemyJobStore(url='sqlite:///jobs.db')}; scheduler = BackgroundScheduler(jobstores=jobstores) — persist jobs across restarts; agent scheduled tasks survive process restart; SQLAlchemy store supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite

Agent async scheduling — from apscheduler.schedulers.asyncio import AsyncIOScheduler; scheduler = AsyncIOScheduler(); scheduler.add_job(async_agent_task, 'interval', seconds=30); scheduler.start() — async jobs run in existing asyncio event loop; agent FastAPI background tasks use AsyncIOScheduler; no separate thread pool needed for async jobs
not_for: Distributed job scheduling — APScheduler is in-process; for distributed job queues across workers use Celery, RQ, or Dramatiq
High-volume job queues — APScheduler is for periodic/scheduled tasks not high-throughput job processing; for task queues use RQ or Celery
Production cron replacement — APScheduler requires Python process to be running; system cron (crontab) is more reliable for critical scheduled tasks
best_when: Adding scheduled/periodic tasks to an existing Python application without external infrastructure — APScheduler runs inside your process and uses the same Python environment as your application.
avoid_when: You need distributed scheduling across multiple workers (use Celery/RQ), high-throughput job queues, or critical tasks that must survive process restarts without external job store.

auth.notes: No auth for in-process scheduling. Job store backends (SQLAlchemy, Redis, MongoDB) use their own auth.

pricing.notes: APScheduler is MIT licensed. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits. Job firing latency: ~1-10ms. BackgroundScheduler uses thread pool — thread creation overhead for each job execution. AsyncIOScheduler has lower overhead for async jobs. Scheduler wakeup resolution: 1 second by default.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SchedulerNotRunningError when adding jobs before scheduler.start(). ConflictingIdError when adding duplicate job ID. JobLookupError for missing job ID. Job exceptions logged but not propagated to caller by default. JobExecutionEvent.exception contains job exception if any. Error messages are clear for scheduler state issues.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: add_job() is not idempotent — calling twice creates duplicate jobs unless replace_existing=True. Job execution is as idempotent as the underlying function. Job stores persist jobs — adding same job after restart requires replace_existing=True.
known_agent_gotchas: Scheduler must be started before adding jobs with some stores — BackgroundScheduler() followed by add_job() then scheduler.start() works; some job store configurations require start() first; agent initialization order: create scheduler, start scheduler, add jobs; or use scheduler.add_job() with jobstore configured before start

Job exceptions are swallowed silently by default — if job function raises exception, APScheduler logs it but does not propagate; agent code needs explicit error handling: scheduler.add_listener(on_job_error, EVENT_JOB_ERROR) where on_job_error checks event.exception; otherwise agent jobs fail silently without alerting

coalesce=True loses missed executions — if scheduler misses 5 firings (process was down), coalesce=True runs job once on resume; coalesce=False runs it 5 times; agent daily reports: coalesce=True (run once when back); agent data sync: coalesce=False might be desired; default is coalesce=True — understand the behavior for your use case

AsyncIOScheduler must run in existing event loop — AsyncIOScheduler().start() must be called from running asyncio event loop; agent FastAPI startup: @app.on_event('startup') async def startup(): scheduler.start(); adding scheduler.start() outside async context raises RuntimeError; BlockingScheduler and BackgroundScheduler do not have this constraint

replace_existing=False is default — scheduler.add_job(fn, id='my_job') called twice raises ConflictingIdError; agent restart code must use: scheduler.add_job(fn, id='my_job', replace_existing=True); or check scheduler.get_job('my_job') before adding; persisted job stores from previous run conflict with new add_job calls on startup

BackgroundScheduler thread dies silently on exception in scheduler thread — if APScheduler internal thread raises unhandled exception, background scheduling stops silently; agent must add: scheduler.add_listener(on_scheduler_error, EVENT_SCHEDULER_STARTED | EVENT_SCHEDULER_SHUTDOWN) to detect scheduler thread death; production agents need health check that verifies scheduler is still firing

security_score_components.security_notes: In-process scheduler with no auth. Job functions execute with process permissions — privilege escalation via scheduled job is a concern if job functions are user-configurable. Job stores (SQLAlchemy, Redis) should use encrypted connections and proper auth. Job arguments stored in job store — avoid storing secrets in job args; use environment variables or secret manager references instead.
